project.name: Morpheus
project.description: Local repo-based agent orchestration system for explainable software work across Beads lifecycle state, GitLab review artifacts, container-backed agents, and run ledgers.

nodes.name: Agent-Ready Contract
nodes.summary: Structured prep output that must explain current behavior, desired behavior, interfaces, acceptance criteria, verification, risk, blockers, and HITL decisions before implementation runs.

nodes.name: Run Ledger
nodes.summary: SQLite-backed audit model with mutable run summaries and immutable ordered run events for operator inspection.

nodes.name: Lane Scheduler
nodes.summary: Pure scheduling boundary that derives runnable preparation, implementation, and review lane work from Beads issue state and configured concurrency.

nodes.name: Adapter Boundary
nodes.summary: Effect runtime ports isolate shell, GitLab, Beads, SQLite, workspace, and container effects from pure core logic and use cases.

layers.name: Product and Domain Docs
layers.description: Product intent, vocabulary, architecture, ADRs, and agent operating instructions.

layers.name: Core Domain
layers.description: Pure TypeScript state machine, scheduler, contracts, and review artifact behavior.

layers.name: Runtime Orchestration
layers.description: Effect use cases, service ports, config, daemon, preparation, implementation, review, and status flows.

layers.name: Adapters
layers.description: Concrete implementations for Beads, GitLab, SQLite, process, workspace, and container agent execution.

layers.name: Operator CLI
layers.description: Command entrypoints and terminal rendering used by maintainers and target repositories.

layers.name: Tests and Fixtures
layers.description: Vitest coverage plus anonymized fixture target repository used for public validation.

layers.name: Tooling and Config
layers.description: Workspace, TypeScript, Vitest, Docker, package, and install configuration.

tour.title: Product Contract
tour.description: Start with product intent, vocabulary, and the explainability principle. These docs define what Morpheus may call public behavior and which target-repo data must stay private.

tour.title: Architecture Decisions
tour.description: Read the architecture brief and ADRs to understand package ownership, Effect v3, Beads lifecycle state, GitLab MR review artifacts, run ledger, and polling daemon lanes.

tour.title: Pure Core
tour.description: Inspect core before runtime changes. Core owns deterministic issue-state transitions, lane scheduling, contract types, and review artifact rendering without Effect dependencies.

tour.title: Effect Runtime
tour.description: Runtime turns pure decisions into orchestrated workflows through Effect service ports. Use this layer for prepare, implementation, review, daemon, status, slice, config, and health behavior.

tour.title: Adapters and Ledger
tour.description: Adapters contain concrete side effects and should preserve runtime port boundaries. SQLite ledger, Beads, GitLab, process, workspace, and agent runner tests show expected observable behavior.

tour.title: Operator Surface
tour.description: CLI package is the human-facing edge. Keep command rendering thin and push workflow behavior into runtime use cases.

tour.title: Public Validation
tour.description: Use anonymized fixtures and top-level smoke tests for public validation. Do not commit private target names, hostnames, URLs, tokens, auth paths, or signoff evidence.
